summary.text: The question is: "Best treatment for melasma near Houston."

action.query: melasma treatment options hydroquinone alternatives Houston

content.text:
**Melasma is a common, chronic form of hyperpigmentation** (often triggered by sun exposure, hormones like pregnancy or birth control, and genetics) that causes brown or grayish patches, typically on the face. It has no permanent cure, but effective management combines strict sun protection, topical/lightening agents, and sometimes procedures. Results vary by skin type, melasma depth (epidermal vs. dermal), and consistency; recurrence is common without ongoing maintenance. Always consult a board-certified dermatologist for personalized diagnosis and treatment, as self-treatment (especially with strong agents or lasers) can worsen it.[[1]](https://www.villagedermatologyhouston.com/melasma)[[2]](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12783984/)

### Core Evidence-Based Treatments
These draw from recent dermatological consensus and studies (as of 2025–2026 data):

- **Daily broad-spectrum sun protection** (foundation of all treatment): Use SPF 30–50+ with iron oxides or tinted formulations to block UVA/UVB and visible light. Reapply every 2 hours outdoors; wear hats/sunglasses. This prevents worsening and supports other therapies.[[3]](https://www.sebastianpodlipnik.com/en/melasma-treatment/)

- **Topical therapies** (first-line for most):
  - **Triple-combination cream** (hydroquinone 4% + tretinoin + mild corticosteroid, e.g., fluocinolone) — often considered the gold standard under medical supervision. Use in cycles (typically 8–12 weeks on, then a break of 1–3 months) to minimize side effects like irritation or ochronosis.[[4]](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40996222/)[[1]](https://www.villagedermatologyhouston.com/melasma)
  - **Hydroquinone alternatives or adjuncts**: Cysteamine (e.g., Cyspera), tranexamic acid (topical), niacinamide, kojic acid, ascorbic acid (vitamin C), or thiamidol. These often have better safety profiles for longer-term or maintenance use.[[5]](https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/09546634.2025.2591502)[[6]](https://yourclearskin.com/melasma-skin-discoloration/)
  - Compounded or prescription options tailored to your skin.

- **Oral tranexamic acid (TXA)**: Low-dose (e.g., 250–500 mg/day) can help refractory cases by targeting underlying vascular/inflammatory factors, with good evidence for adjunctive use under supervision.[[7]](https://rohtoaohalclinic.com.vn/en/best-melasma-treatments-in-2025-dermatologist-approved-solutions-for-clearer-skin.html)[[8]](https://www.sonderbeaut.com/best-melasma-treatments-2025/)

- **In-office procedures** (adjunctive, not standalone; choose carefully):
  - Chemical peels (e.g., VI Peel), microneedling (sometimes with exosomes/serums), or specific lasers (e.g., Clear + Brilliant, Halo hybrid, PicoWay, Spectra). Lasers/peels carry risks of rebound pigmentation if not suited to your skin type—only under expert supervision.[[9]](https://www.dermlasersurgery.com/skin-conditions/melasma/)[[10]](https://houstonskin.com/dermatology/melasma-houston-tx/)[[11]](https://www.aestheticspecialistshtx.com/melasma-treatment-bellaire-tx/)
  - Combinations (e.g., topicals + procedures) often yield better results than monotherapy.

Lifestyle factors like avoiding hormone triggers (when possible) and consistent routines are key.

### Recommended Providers Near Houston
Houston has several board-certified dermatologists and practices experienced with melasma. Search for those emphasizing laser expertise or comprehensive pigment management:

- **Dermatology & Laser Surgery Center (Dr. Paul M. Friedman)**: Highly regarded for laser treatments (e.g., Spectra, PicoWay, Vbeam) and topicals; extensive experience with melasma. Location in central Houston.[[9]](https://www.dermlasersurgery.com/skin-conditions/melasma/)[[12]](https://www.dermlasersurgery.com/our-providers/cosmetic-dermatologist/)
- **Elite Dermatology**: Multiple Greater Houston locations (Fulshear, Kingwood, etc.); offers topicals, oral TXA, microneedling, and peels.[[13]](https://www.elitedermatology.com/services/melasma/)
- **Village Dermatology (Katy & Memorial/Houston)**: Focuses on triple creams, cycling hydroquinone, sun protection, and cautious procedural use.[[1]](https://www.villagedermatologyhouston.com/melasma)
- **Aviva Dermatology**: Personalized topicals, orals, and in-office options.[[14]](https://www.avivaderm.com/melasma/)
- **Houston Skin (Dr. Stephen K. Tyring and team)**: Peels, Halo laser, and pigment-specific care (locations in Houston area, Pearland, Webster).[[10]](https://houstonskin.com/dermatology/melasma-houston-tx/)

Other options include Aesthetic Specialists of Houston (Clear + Brilliant packages with peels/microneedling) and practices offering Cyspera or compounded therapies.[[11]](https://www.aestheticspecialistshtx.com/melasma-treatment-bellaire-tx/)

**Next steps**: Schedule a consultation with a dermatologist for skin evaluation (sometimes Wood’s lamp or biopsy to assess depth). Bring up your skin type, triggers, and prior treatments. Expect gradual improvement over weeks to months, with follow-ups for maintenance. Prices and availability vary—check insurance for medical aspects and reviews for cosmetic ones. This is not medical advice; professional evaluation is essential.
